question: Describe the characteristics of the Operating Floor.

nuscale_ch01_0110.title: Figure 1.7-1b: Electrical Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0110.summary: This section provides a comprehensive legend of electrical symbols and abbreviations used in NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report drawings, including representations for switches, relays, indicators, and logic gates. It explains the meaning of each symbol and describes the logic functions (AND, OR, NOT, bistable outputs) used in instrumentation and control diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0354.title: SRP 9.3.3, Rev 3: Equipment and Floor Drainage SystemII.1 Protection Against Natural Phenomena
nuscale_ch01_0354.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale equipment and floor drainage system meets the regulatory criteria for protection against natural phenomena, with full conformance noted and no exceptions. The system is designed to withstand environmental and dynamic effects, and it does not allow any portion of the drain system to penetrate the containment barrier, ensuring control of radioactive material releases.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing reactor operators to monitor and control multiple NPMs using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive plant information, alarms, and controls for effective monitoring and diagnostics.

nuscale_ch01_0355.title: SRP 9.3.3, Rev 3: Equipment and Floor Drainage SystemII.2 Environmental and Dynamic Effects
nuscale_ch01_0355.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale equipment and floor drainage system meets the regulatory requirements for protection against environmental and dynamic effects, with no exceptions noted. It states that the system conforms to the relevant criteria, ensuring safety and compliance with established standards.

edges.description: Section A addresses protection of the operating floor against natural phenomena, while Section B addresses the operating floor's response to environmental and dynamic effects, both contributing specific safety-related characteristics relevant to the analysis question.

Section A describes the location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room, which is below grade and not on the Operating Floor, while Section B discusses regulatory criteria for equipment and floor drainage systems relevant to various plant areas, including the Operating Floor, thus Section A provides contextual information that helps distinguish the Operating Floor from other plant areas referenced in Section B.
